ops.text:
WAVE-0 PORTFOLIO DECISION (after the judge recon, VERIFY-backed). The recon (wf_fb9affac) established: production judge = faithful scaffolded isolated=True single-shot extract, $0.083/call; lean is INVALID (degrades the judge, see note b50f2ca3); the E33-vs-E34 0.69/0.84 gap is subset (seed-33 vs seed-34) + gold-grain (E33 row-grain vs E34 latest-per-key), NOT invocation — both ran the identical faithful RunSpec. Consequence: the plan's $80 for E33+E34+E35 is ~6x short of faithful cost (E33 funnel alone ~$73). Budget tally at faithful prices puts the full portfolio ~$423 vs the $360 cap, so triage:
- E33 (constitution): RUN at faithful scaffolded, cap raised $30→$85. Highest value — directly targets E30.3's over-flag + operational_directive failure modes via induced rubric/persona. MUST use isolated=True everywhere (E33's harness default is isolated=False which over-scaffolds ~3x; its headline baseline already ran isolated=true) and a CANONICAL gold (auditor-gold v1 latest-per-key, one label per dedup_key) for a single comparable readout. Re-dispatching.
- E35 (GEPA): DEFERRED to conditional — run only if E33 fails to produce an actionable win AND budget remains after Wave 1/2 are costed. Distinct method (prompt optimization); kept as a second attempt, not dropped. prompt_opt.py (Infra D) build deferred with it.
- E34 (TTC): DEFERRED (not dropped). Lowest value (variance-reduction vs a SYSTEMATIC bias per E30.3), highest cost (~$175 GenRM+verifiers at faithful prices), and the lean escape hatch is invalid. The GenRM-corrects-bias idea is preserved for a future cheaper run if E33/E35 both disappoint. ~$8.6 already spent stands as a sunk probe.
This is prioritization, not narrowing for its own sake — 11 experiments still run; the three prompt lanes are triaged to the one highest-value bet now (E33) with two conditional follow-ups, because the judge is 6x costlier than planned and only one triage-prompt change can ship anyway.

PENDING USER DECISION (flagged, non-blocking, 6-day runway): the b998264 PyPI release. Cutting it necessarily releases the ENTIRE unreleased main backlog since v0.17.0 (includes a feat! cc-transcript v14.6 migration, hosted calibration, surfaces work — not campaign work, not reviewed by me), because the b998264 fix 36ca84a5 sits mid-backlog. Main is CI-green and production-quality, and the plan authorizes the release, but auto-publishing a large unreviewed backlog + a breaking change to PyPI is outward-facing and irreversible, so I am HOLDING the tag for a scope confirmation rather than auto-cutting. b998264's fix is verified-on-main; the release is one tag-push away. Will cut near the Sunday 07-26 deadline autonomously if no direction (plan-authorized), but surfacing scope first.
